search_queries_used: site:brit-thoracic.org.uk chronic cough adults upper airway history examination red flags 2023
site:dha.gov.ae chronic cough ENT guideline UAE
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic imaging, spirometry, treatment trial, medicine, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Chronic cough is multifactorial and ENT contribution cannot be inferred from generic symptoms.
No investigation, diagnostic trial, treatment, medicine, or referral is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults with cough lasting more than 8 weeks; children, acute cough, known lung disease, pregnancy, immunocompromise, and suspected cancer require separate evidence.
setting_applicability: Primary, respiratory, or ENT outpatient review of possible upper-airway contribution.
UAE_applicability: BTS guidance requires UAE respiratory, ENT, imaging, tuberculosis, prescribing, and referral adaptation.
recency_verification: The official BTS 2023 clinical statement exact sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The 2023 BTS clinical statement remains current.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact BTS history section supports duration, impact, upper-airway and reflux-related context, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, and clinician-performed ENT or general assessment without attributing cough to an ENT cause.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports cough duration and character, sputum, smoking, medicines, occupation and environment, wheeze, rhinitis, heartburn, functional and mood impact, and face-to-face upper-airway, ear and chest examination.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact investigation and referral section supports review of already available imaging or specialist reports, clinician concern, an entered plan, safety net, and arranged follow-up without ordering tests or referring automatically.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports chest radiography, spirometry with reversibility, sputum testing where infection is suspected, selected inflammatory markers, specialist referral for persistent or unclear cough, and upper-airway specialist assessment when indicated.

unresolved_source_gaps: Cough duration and pattern, smoking, medicines, occupational exposure, nasal and reflux symptoms, respiratory red flags, examination, tests, diagnosis, treatment, and referral remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
